connectors.purpose: Primary output standard for all prose in the ranking — no em dashes, no lists of three, contractions throughout, varied sentence length. Must be read before drafting any section and applied to the list introduction, entry content, and verdicts equally.

Defines the publishing lifecycle this skill follows. Phases 1–7 map to the execution flow: keyword research, content planning, writing, technical, on-page SEO, publishing, and post-publish. Compliance is a hard requirement.

Keeps the ranking audience-aware. The reader landed here from a category search query — they're choosing between options, not evaluating a single game. Every entry should speak to that decision context.
Provides the question pool for the FAQ section. Ranked list FAQs should be list-level questions about the category or how to choose — game-specific FAQs belong on individual game pages.

dynamic_connectors.note: Load the author persona file that matches the 'author' input before writing. The author persona defines voice and what the author notices — it informs the list introduction and reviewer attribution line.

source_drafts.note: Read all game page draft files provided in the 'draft_paths' input. These are not fixed connectors — they vary per ranking. Extract game name, overall score, metric scores, Pros, Cons, Who Is This Game For, and Verdict from each draft before drafting begins.
